objective: Plan the org-wide active repository governance coverage epic so every active NIBARGERB-HLDPRO repository is represented in governance, explicitly classified, and covered by an end-to-end validation gate before closeout.
scope_boundary: Create the issue-backed planning package for epic #298 before implementation starts.
Define the canonical policy that active org repos must be present in the governed repository registry even when exempt or limited-scope.
Define acceptance criteria for registry expansion, live org drift detection, seek-and-ponder intake, EmailAssistant discovery, dependent surface reconciliation, and final e2e proof.
Keep this slice planning-only except for status mirror updates in governance.
Do not edit downstream repositories from this planning slice.
out_of_scope: Editing seek-and-ponder, EmailAssistant, or any other downstream repo in this slice.
Adding implementation code for drift detection in this planning package.
Changing STANDARDS.md tier semantics before alternate-family review and implementation scope are accepted.
Claiming governance coverage complete before live org inventory e2e proof exists.
Closing epic #298 from this planning package.
research_summary: Live GitHub org inventory on 2026-04-18 returned eight active repos: hldpro-governance, ai-integration-services, HealthcarePlatform, knocktracker, ASC-Evaluator, local-ai-machine, seek-and-ponder, and EmailAssistant. Current governance registry, standards, README, graphify targets, and sweep surfaces cover only six canonical repos. seek-and-ponder already has local governance-front-door files and core docs, while EmailAssistant is visible in GitHub but not present as a local checkout under ~/Developer/HLDPRO. The durable fix is to make registry presence mandatory for every active org repo and treat exemption as an explicit classification rather than absence.

sprints.name: Epic Planning And Policy
sprints.goal: Create the canonical issue-backed plan and acceptance criteria for org-wide active repo governance coverage.
tasks: Create GitHub epic #298 before local planning artifacts.
Record the policy that all active org repos must be present in docs/governed_repos.json.
Define tier options for full, standard, infra/runtime, knowledge, archived, and exempt repositories.
Add planning artifacts and status mirror rows.
Validate the planning package locally.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub issue #298 exists and is labeled as an epic governance issue.
Structured plan and PDCA/R companion cite #298.
Acceptance criteria include live org inventory, registry coverage, intake, dependent surface reconciliation, and final e2e testing.
Planning scope forbids downstream repo edits.
Backlog and progress mirrors reference #298 while active.

sprints.name: Org Inventory Drift Detector
sprints.goal: Make live GitHub org inventory drift mechanically visible against the governed repository registry.
tasks: Add a validator that compares active non-archived NIBARGERB-HLDPRO repos with docs/governed_repos.json.
Extend the governed repo registry schema and validator so lifecycle and exemption classifications are machine-checkable.
Fail or warn when an active repo is absent from the registry.
Fail or warn when a registry entry points to a missing or archived repo without an accepted exemption.
Add tests for missing active repo, archived repo, and exact-match cases.
Wire the detector into weekly sweep first, then make CI enforcement explicit once current known gaps are closed.
acceptance_criteria: Live org inventory can be captured with repo name, archived state, default branch, privacy, URL, and pushedAt.
Registry schema can represent active, archived, exempt, limited-scope, and adoption-blocked states without relying on prose-only notes.
Exempt or adoption-blocked entries require owner, rationale, review date, and issue-backed deferral evidence.
Known active repo omissions are reported deterministically.
Archived and intentionally exempt repos are classified, not silently ignored.
Weekly sweep surfaces drift with enough detail to open issue-backed intake work.
CI or Local CI has a stable offline fixture path for tests.

sprints.name: seek-and-ponder Intake
sprints.goal: Classify seek-and-ponder and add it to governance coverage without changing product behavior.
tasks: Classify seek-and-ponder governance and security tiers.
Add seek-and-ponder to docs/governed_repos.json with explicit subsystem flags.
Reconcile graphify target, wiki path, project path, compendium, raw feed, metrics, Codex ingestion, and memory integrity expectations.
Verify existing CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CODEX.md, PROGRESS, FAIL_FAST_LOG, FEATURE_REGISTRY, DATA_DICTIONARY, and SERVICE_REGISTRY against the shared contract.
Create downstream issue(s) before any seek-and-ponder repo edits are needed.
acceptance_criteria: seek-and-ponder is present in docs/governed_repos.json with governance tier, security tier, GitHub repo, local path, graph output path, wiki path, project path, and subsystem flags.
seek-and-ponder is represented in README/STANDARDS governed repo surfaces or those surfaces are generated/validated from the registry.
seek-and-ponder graphify and wiki coverage is either produced or explicitly deferred with an issue-backed reason.
Baseline security and governance doc checks are run against seek-and-ponder.
No downstream seek-and-ponder edits happen without a downstream issue and execution scope.

sprints.name: EmailAssistant Discovery And Classification
sprints.goal: Inspect EmailAssistant and decide its governance tier before adoption work starts.
tasks: Clone or inspect EmailAssistant default branch in an isolated worktree.
Identify stack, data sensitivity, repo activity, CI state, and existing governance docs.
Classify governance and security tiers.
Create a downstream adoption issue if repo-local governance files or CI need changes.
Add registry coverage once classification is accepted.
acceptance_criteria: EmailAssistant is no longer absent from governance by accident.
EmailAssistant is either added to docs/governed_repos.json or has an issue-backed classification blocker with owner, rationale, and next step.
Municipal/email data sensitivity is considered before security tier selection.
Any missing local checkout or authentication blocker is captured as evidence, not treated as exemption.
No EmailAssistant edits happen without a downstream issue and execution scope.

sprints.name: Registry-Driven Surface Reconciliation
sprints.goal: Remove or guard hardcoded repo lists so future active org repos cannot silently fall out of governance coverage.
tasks: Reconcile README and STANDARDS governed repo tables with docs/governed_repos.json.
Reconcile docs/graphify_targets.json with registry graphify-enabled repos or document a generated path.
Reconcile overlord-sweep, overlord-nightly-cleanup, raw-feed-sync, metrics, compendium, and memory-integrity consumers with registry-driven repo selection.
Reconcile org ruleset, branch protection, default branch, and required-check expectations for newly included active repos.
Add tests that catch registry/graphify/sweep selection drift.
Document accepted exceptions where workflow checkout mechanics still require explicit entries.
acceptance_criteria: Every duplicate repo list is either generated from the registry, validated against it, or documented as an intentional workflow limitation.
Graphify target reconciliation passes for all graphify-enabled repos.
Sweep, metrics, compendium, and raw-feed repo selection are tested against the expanded registry.
Newly included active repos have branch protection, default branch, org ruleset applicability, and required-check coverage validated or explicitly deferred by issue.
README and STANDARDS cannot drift silently from the registry.
Any deferred hardcoded surface has an issue-backed follow-up.

sprints.name: Final E2E Governance Coverage Gate
sprints.goal: Make e2e testing the final acceptance criterion before epic #298 can close.
tasks: Run live GitHub org inventory drift detection against docs/governed_repos.json.
Run governed repo registry schema validation.
Run graphify target reconciliation.
Run registry-backed sweep or a faithful local sweep selection simulation.
Run compendium/metrics/raw-feed selection tests for the expanded repo set.
Run the final e2e command matrix and attach exact output to the closeout.
Run baseline governance checks against newly included repos or record accepted deferrals.
Create closeout evidence and attach links/results to #298.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: live GitHub org inventory and docs/governed_repos.json agree on every active repo or list explicit accepted classifications.
Final AC: docs/governed_repos.json validates against docs/schemas/governed-repos.schema.json.
Final AC: lifecycle/exemption/adoption-blocked classifications are schema-valid and include owner, rationale, review date, and issue evidence when required.
Final AC: graphify target reconciliation passes for all graphify-enabled repos.
Final AC: sweep, metrics, compendium, raw-feed, memory-integrity, and Codex-ingestion selection paths are registry-driven or have documented accepted exceptions.
Final AC: branch protection, default branch, org ruleset applicability, and required checks are validated for every newly included active repo or deferred by linked issue.

Final AC: a named command matrix covers live org inventory, registry/schema validation, graphify reconciliation, sweep selection, compendium generation/check, raw-feed selection, memory integrity selection, Codex-ingestion selection, required-check/ruleset validation, and baseline governance checks for new repos.
Final AC: seek-and-ponder governance baseline passes or has issue-backed deferrals.
Final AC: EmailAssistant classification is complete and adoption blockers are issue-backed.
Final AC: final closeout cites e2e command output, GitHub issue links, and any remaining accepted deferrals.

specialist_reviews.focus: Ensure the epic handles active org repo coverage as a registry policy problem rather than a one-off seek-and-ponder patch.
specialist_reviews.summary: The plan should make registry presence mandatory for active org repos, classify exemption explicitly, and require final live inventory e2e proof before closure. Follow-up implementation must still obtain any required alternate-family review for standards changes.
evidence: gh repo list NIBARGERB-HLDPRO output from 2026-04-18

specialist_reviews.focus: Repo-rule compliance, truthful alternate-review state, schema-enforced classification, branch/ruleset ACs, and final e2e command matrix.
specialist_reviews.summary: Flagged that the first draft overstated alternate-family review acceptance and lacked explicit machine-checkable classification, branch/ruleset/required-check ACs, and a concrete final e2e command matrix. This revision incorporates those corrections and leaves true alternate-family review as a future implementation/standards gate.

alternate_model_review.summary: No true alternate-family review is claimed for this planning package. Implementation PRs that change standards, registry enforcement, schema semantics, or org-wide workflow behavior must carry the required cross-family review artifact before merge.
evidence: Operator instruction on 2026-04-18 to proceed through PDCA/R, GitHub epic, ACs, and e2e final AC
Planning-only scope boundary in this structured plan
Zeno subagent finding that alternate-review acceptance must remain truthful until real evidence exists

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #298 may add this planning package, execution-scope artifact, and governance status mirror rows only. This readiness authorizes child issue planning, not registry/workflow/downstream implementation.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Review and merge this planning package, then open child issue-backed implementation slices for drift detection, seek-and-ponder intake, EmailAssistant discovery, registry-driven workflow reconciliation, and final e2e closeout.

material_deviation_rules: If another active org repo appears during live inventory, add it to #298 scope before closeout or create an issue-backed classification follow-up.
If seek-and-ponder requires product-repo edits, stop and create a downstream issue and execution scope before writing there.
If EmailAssistant cannot be inspected, record the authentication or checkout blocker and keep #298 open.
If a workflow cannot be registry-driven because of GitHub Actions checkout constraints, document the exception and add a validator that catches drift.
If registry classification remains prose-only, do not claim the active-repo coverage policy is enforceable.
If branch protection, default branch, ruleset, or required-check coverage is skipped for newly included repos, record a linked deferral before closeout.
If no final e2e command matrix exists, do not close #298.
If final e2e live inventory proof is skipped, do not mark #298 done.
If standards semantics change, require cross-family review before merge.
approved_by: operator instruction: follow repo rules and loop through pdcar, gh epic, ACs, e2e testing as final AC
